Here are the search results for IBM Maximo maintenance scheduling visualization UX patterns interface:

## IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduling Visualization & UX Patterns

### Native Maximo Graphical Applications

Graphical Planning and Scheduling applications have been in Maximo (and now Manage) for quite some time. The Graphical apps embedded into Maximo, first implemented as a module to activate, and now just part of the base product, sought to provide comprehensive planning and scheduling tools directly in Maximo.(no content)You can plan and schedule upcoming work in a graphical view.

Key native applications include:

- **Graphical Work Week**: You use the Graphical Work Week application to schedule and assign work while you manage resources over a short period of time. Being able to visualize the week ahead and publish (print/export) as needed are helpful features when you just need a quick view of scheduled work.

- **Graphical Resource View**: The Graphical Resource View application is used to manage resource hours over a period. In the graphical view, scheduled work time per shift can be seen, as well as modified availability in a single view. You can see total resource hours by craft, and access assignment records.

### Third-Party UX Solutions

**Naviam Planner**: Naviam Planner simplifies the process by providing an intuitive, drag-and-drop interface for scheduling work orders, assigning resources, and balancing workloads. Easily assign work orders to the right technicians, shifts, or teams with a simple, visual interface.(no content)Quickly schedule tasks by dragging work orders onto technician schedules in an interactive, calendar-based interface. Choose between daily, weekly, or monthly views to better manage short-term tasks and long-term planning.

Here are the search results for "SAP PM EAM scheduling optimization visualization dashboard":

## SAP PM/EAM Scheduling Optimization and Visualization Dashboards

### Key Solutions and Approaches

**Prometheus Group Planning & Scheduling**
Prometheus Group offers a solution that lets you visualize your maintenance work and resources within a user-friendly graphical scheduler. You can drag-and-drop, Direct Edit, or Mass Change your orders and operations, while seeing those effects on resource capacity and availability in real-time.(no content)Their solution allows you to comprehensively manage preventive maintenance and routine maintenance items in one view to optimize schedules and improve operational performance. It enhances scheduling with features like Weighted Priority, Auto Level, and Manual Percent Complete.

**Innovapptive SAP Maintenance Planning & Scheduling**
Innovapptive's solution helps streamline work orders, automate scheduling, and optimize resources to minimize downtime and maximize asset performance, giving schedulers a clear, at-a-glance view of workloads by year, month, and week.(no content)The solution simplifies scheduling with a drag-and-drop interface built for speed, allowing users to visualize maintenance tasks, workforce, and resources with an easy-to-use graphical scheduler.

**SAP S/4HANA Resource Scheduling**
SAP Community resources discuss how to handle resource scheduling for maintenance orders that are part of Phase Based Maintenance framework, including for organizations following phase-based maintenance for reactive and proactive maintenance.

**LeverX SAP EAM Solutions**
SAP EAM solutions for resource allocation and scheduling optimize the use of workforce, equipment, and materials across projects. These solutions help balance resource utilization to minimize downtime and maximize productivity, and enhance scheduling accuracy with real-time updates and integrated planning.(no content)LeverX also offers real-time, spatial visualization of linear assets with location analytics and optimized work scheduling with remote monitoring capabilities.

### Integration and Analytics

Based on my search, here are the relevant findings about railway maintenance scheduling UIs at Deutsche Bahn (and limited results for MTR):

## Deutsche Bahn Railway Maintenance Scheduling Systems

### Fleet Employment Optimization (FEO) System
Deutsche Bahn (DB) operates a large fleet of rolling stock (locomotives, wagons, and train sets) that must be combined into trains to perform rolling stock rotations. This train composition is a special characteristic of railway operations that distinguishes rolling stock rotation planning from vehicle scheduling problems in other industries.(no content)FEO offers interactive planning options that are implemented via a drag-and-drop user interface, including filtering, and embedded reporting functionality.(no content)FEO mitigates problems by detecting and reporting issues as lists of errors or warnings that describe the problem found (what) and provide a reference to the input (where) using railway terminology, such as train numbers and station names. FEO includes powerful editors that allow the postprocessing of complex data sets.

### Steuerung Gleiswechsel App
With the Steuerung Gleiswechsel app, dispatchers can see at any time where trains are located, whether stabling sidings are still available or whether shunting movements are possible.(no content)Thanks to this app, track change times are reduced by 40 per cent. This reduces waiting times for drivers and speeds up train provision.

### AI-Based Maintenance Scheduling
DB is currently developing an AI-based tool that uses data-driven forecasting to enable S-Bahn maintenance planners to schedule wheelset replacement and conditioning more effectively, thus saving on resources and costs and helping to increase vehicle availability.(no content)AI supports the fleet management process to achieve optimal planning and management of maintenance stops. Thereby the capacity of the depots is optimized and maintenance measures are prioritized through digital fleet management.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about maintenance optimization, visualization metrics, and before/after results:

## Maintenance Optimization Metrics and Visualization

### Key Maintenance Metrics to Track

Maintenance performance metrics and KPIs help you track your organization's success at meeting its goals, such as cutting costs or reducing downtime.(no content)Modern software lets you easily transform performance data into meaningful reports and dashboards, giving your organization both quantitative and qualitative insight into your maintenance strategy.

### Important KPIs for Before/After Comparison

1. **Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)**: Mean time between failures refers to the average time between breakdowns. This metric is used to analyze the equipment design and safety of complex assets and is based on the ratio of operational hours to the number of failures.

2. **Mean Time to Repair (MTTR)**: MTTR measures the average time it takes to repair equipment and return it to operational status after a failure. This critical maintenance performance indicator directly impacts equipment availability.

3. **Planned Maintenance Percentage (PMP)**: Tracking planned vs. unplanned maintenance helps you measure the success of your program. Knowing what fraction of all maintenance tasks are planned helps you identify inefficient processes. This maintenance metric is expressed as the percent of the total number of hours spent on planned tasks in a specified period. A value above 90% is considered "world class maintenance," while a value over 70% is considered acceptable.

Here are the search results for fleet maintenance management Gantt chart visualization in the rail industry:

## Fleet Maintenance Management with Gantt Chart Visualization

### General Fleet Maintenance and Gantt Charts

Gantt charts can be used to schedule and track fleet maintenance activities, including routine inspections, repairs, and servicing. By visualizing maintenance schedules, transportation companies can ensure that their vehicles are well-maintained.(no content)Gantt charts can be used to manage and optimize the use of resources such as personnel, vehicles, and equipment. By visualizing resource availability and scheduling, transportation companies can ensure that resources are utilized effectively and avoid bottlenecks or underutilization.

### Advanced Gantt-Based Fleet Management Solutions

A U.S.-based sea cruise operator swapped hundreds of messy spreadsheets and a legacy scheduling system with limited functionality for a custom DHTMLX Gantt–powered unified fleet management system. Now, ships, crews, bookings, and maintenance all run on one screen — with real-time updates, conflict prevention, and nearly 20% fewer idle hours.(no content)While most users interact with Gantt charts in a single-screen view, this solution introduced multi-layered visualization. One improvement is the vertical highlighting of the bar across its active dates. A more advanced feature allows multiple task bars to be overlaid in the same row, with the row height expanding dynamically.

### Rail Industry Fleet Management
